Student Life

A Vibrant Community Where Students Thrive

Boarding Life

A home away from home where students learn independence, responsibility, and community living

A Supportive Environment

Our boarding program provides a safe, structured, and nurturing environment where students can focus on their studies while developing life skills and lasting friendships.

  • ✓ Comfortable, well-maintained dormitories with appropriate supervision
  • ✓ Structured daily routines that balance study, rest, and recreation
  • ✓ Dedicated boarding staff who care for students' physical and emotional well-being
  • ✓ Evening study sessions with academic support
  • ✓ Weekend activities and outings
  • ✓ Regular communication with parents and guardians

Clubs & Sports

Opportunities for every student to explore interests, develop talents, and build friendships

Academic Clubs

  • • Science Club
  • • Mathematics Club
  • • Debate Club
  • • Reading Club
  • • Chess Club

Creative Arts

  • • Music Club
  • • Art & Craft
  • • Drama Club
  • • Choir
  • • Poetry Club

Sports

  • • Football
  • • Swimming
  • • Athletics
  • • Volleyball
  • • Netball

Service & Leadership

  • • Student Council
  • • Community Service
  • • Environmental Club
  • • Pathfinders/Scouts

Cultural

  • • Swahili Club
  • • Cultural Dance
  • • Heritage Club

Science & Technology

  • • Computer Club
  • • Young Farmers Club

Clubs meet regularly and participate in competitions, performances, and community events. All students are encouraged to join at least one club that interests them.

Meals & Student Welfare

Nourishing bodies and caring for the whole child

Nutritious Meals

We believe that healthy, balanced meals are essential for student well-being and academic performance. Our kitchen staff prepares fresh, nutritious meals daily.

  • ✓ Three balanced meals per day for boarding students
  • ✓ Mid-morning snack and afternoon tea
  • ✓ Lunch available for day students
  • ✓ Special dietary needs accommodated
  • ✓ Clean, hygienic dining facilities

Student Welfare

The well-being of every student is our priority. We provide comprehensive support to ensure students feel safe, valued, and cared for.

  • ✓ School counselor available for emotional and social support
  • ✓ Health services and first aid on campus
  • ✓ Regular health check-ups and wellness programs
  • ✓ Anti-bullying policies and programs
  • ✓ Open communication channels between school, students, and parents
  • ✓ Safe and secure campus environment

A Day in the Life

6:00 AM

Wake Up & Morning Devotion

Boarding students start the day with prayer and preparation

7:00 AM

Breakfast

Nutritious meal to fuel the day

8:00 AM

Classes Begin

Academic instruction and learning activities

10:30 AM

Break Time

Snack and recreation

1:00 PM

Lunch

Midday meal and rest

2:00 PM

Afternoon Classes

Continued learning and activities

4:00 PM

Clubs & Sports

Extracurricular activities and recreation

6:00 PM

Dinner

Evening meal (boarding students)

7:00 PM

Study Time

Supervised homework and study sessions

9:00 PM

Evening Devotion & Rest

Reflection, prayer, and preparation for rest
